content.problemStatement:
Your favourite cricket player played very well today. He made X(100≤X≤200)X (100 \le X \le 200)X(100≤X≤200) runs from exactly 100100100 balls. Each ball he scored either 000, 111, 222, 333, 444 or 666 runs.

Unfortunately you missed the match. So now, you are wondering what is the maximum number of sixers he could have hit? A sixer is a ball where you score 666 runs.


Input Format

The first and only line of input contains XXX, the total number of runs scored.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum number of 666s.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
The player could have scored 666s on 161616 balls, 444 on 111 ball and then 000 on the remaining 838383 balls.

Explanation:
The player could have scored 666 on 252525 balls, and then 000 on 757575 balls.
